title: Task: Review /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web against its design philosophy. Focus on mismatches between docs and code. Return concise findings with severity, file paths, and suggested fixes. Do not edit files.
summary_text: 1. **High**: Five-view IA in docs is not what the app ships. Evidence: docs require only `Happy/Now/Tasks/Alerts/Recover` ([architecture](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/docs/kernel-happy-app-single-front-architecture.md:230), [README scope](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/README.md:39)); code ships `Home/Work/Live/Recover` ([index](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/index.html:301), [index nav labels](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/index.html:384)); tests enforce current 4-screen model ([test-view-contract](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/tests/test-view-contract.sh:7)). Suggested fix: choose one source of truth, then align both docs and implementation; if docs win, split `Work` into `Now/Tasks/Alerts` and remove `Live` as top-level; if code wins, update README/architecture/pencil brief and rename philosophy language accordingly. 2. **High**: State vocabulary drift (protocol vs architecture spec). Evidence: architecture defines system/task states like `continuity`, `rollback`, `executing`, `waiting-human`, `completed`, `rolled-back` ([architecture](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/docs/kernel-happy-app-single-front-architecture.md:523)); code protocol/filtering is `running`, `needs-human`, `needs-review`, `done` ([event protocol](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/domain/happy-event-protocol.js:6), [task filtering](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/app.js:680)); tests lock this current enum ([behavior test](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/tests/test-behavior-contract.js:82)). Suggested fix: add a canonical mapping layer (or update enums) at adapter boundaries and add contract tests that accept documented status vocabulary from remote payloads. 3. **Medium**: “Mobile bounded intervention” philosophy conflicts with first-class `Live` desktop bridge in primary nav. Evidence: docs position mobile as quick intake/progress/bounded intervention while desktop remains high-context ([architecture](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/docs/kernel-happy-app-single-front-architecture.md:125), [README](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/README.md:13)); UI makes `Live` a primary tab and action path ([index](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/index.html:301), [app live launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/app.js:1013)). Suggested fix: demote `Live` to secondary action behind `Happy/Tasks` (feature-flagged), or explicitly revise philosophy docs to bless direct live-session routing as primary. 4. **Medium**: Design philosophy requires parallel simulation-lane verification, but verification here is single-lane scripts. Evidence: plan still requires “at least three parallel simulation lanes” ([implementation plan](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/docs/kernel-happy-app-implementation-plan.md:18)); current checks are standalone scripts ([design contract](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/tests/test-design-contract.sh:1), [view contract](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/tests/test-view-contract.sh:1)). Suggested fix: add a CI orchestration runner that executes 3 materially distinct lanes (implementation, verification, independent critique) and persists lane evidence artifacts. All current happy-web contract tests passed in this review, but they currently validate the drift above.
